_description: Evaluation scenarios with ground-truth labels. Each entry maps a free-text consult question to the expected ACR topic + variant the system should retrieve, and a list of procedures that should appear in the recommendations with their expected appropriateness scores.

scenarios.scenario: 42-year-old woman with sudden severe headache, peak intensity within seconds, described as worst headache of her life. No prior trauma. What is the appropriate initial imaging?
34-year-old man with three days of mild low back pain after lifting groceries. No fever, no neurologic symptoms, no weight loss, no cancer history.
67-year-old man with low back pain and a history of prostate cancer; new onset of bowel incontinence and saddle anesthesia.
scenarios.expected_top_procedure: MRI lumbar spine without and with IV contrast

scenarios.scenario: 55-year-old man, acute chest pain in ED. Two negative troponins, ECG non-diagnostic. Considered low-to-intermediate probability ACS. What rule-out imaging?
scenarios.expected_top_procedure: CTA coronary arteries with IV contrast

scenarios.scenario: 28-year-old pregnant woman, second trimester, with right lower quadrant pain, fever, leukocytosis. Suspected appendicitis.
8-year-old boy, RLQ pain, fever, vomiting. Suspected appendicitis.
45-year-old woman, dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain. Wells score intermediate. Normal renal function. Suspected pulmonary embolism.
scenarios.expected_top_procedure: CTA chest with IV contrast (PE protocol)

scenarios.scenario: 30-year-old pregnant woman in third trimester, dyspnea and tachycardia, suspected PE.
